senses.gloss: The fourth letter of the Arabic alphabet, called ثَاء (ṯāʔ) and written in the Arabic script; preceded by ت (t) and followed by ج (j).
The twenty-third numeral symbol of the Arabic alphabet (traditional abjad order, used for list numbering), written in the Arabic script; preceded by ت (t) and followed by خ (ḵ).

etymology.text: Derived from ت (t), and originally written as such (in rasm) by analogy with Nabatean tradition, as Nabatean cognates of Arabic words with /θ/ were indiscriminately spelled using 𐢞 (t).

entries.body_html:
ث :الثاء من الحروف اللِّثَوِيَّة، وهي من الحروف المهموسة،

وهي والظاء والذال في حيز واحد.

entries.lexicon_en: Murtaḍa al-Zabīdī, Tāj al-ʿArūs fī Jawāhir al-Qamūs (d. 1790 CE)
entries.body_html:
(بَاب الثاءِ المثلّثة)
وَهِي من الحروفِ اللّثَوهيَّةِ والمَهْمُوسَةِ، وَهِي والظَّاءُ والذّالُ فِي حَيِّزٍ وَاحِد. وَقد أُبْدِلت من الفاءِ فِي حُثَالَة وحُفَالَة، وَمن السّين فِي الجُثْمان والجُسْمَان، وَغير ذَلِك مِمَّا ذكرَه ابنُ السِّكّيتِ، وَابْن السّيد فِي الْفرق، وابنُ فارسٍ وَغَيرهم.

هَكَذَا فِي النُّسَخ، وَفِي بَعْضهَا: (الهَمْزَةِ) بدل (الأَلف) ، وَعَلَيْهَا علامَةُ الصِّحَة.

entries.lexicon_en: Arabic-English Lexicon by Edward William Lane (d. 1876)
entries.lexicon_ar: المعجم العربي الإنجليزي لإدوارد وليام لين
entries.body_html:
ث alphabetical letter ث

The fourth letter of the alphabet: called ثَآءٌ and ثَا [respecting which latter see the letter ب]: the pl. [of the former] is ثَآءَاتٌ; and [of the latter,] أَثْوَآءٌ and أَثْيَآءٌ. (TA in باب الالف اللّينة.) It is one of the letters termed مَهْمُوسَة [or non-vocal, i. e. pronounced with the breath only, without the voice], and of those termed لِثَوِيَّة [or gingival], which are ث and ذ and ظ. (TA at the commencement of ف.)

b2: It is sometimes substituted for ف as in the instance of حُثَالَةٌ and حُفَالَةٌ; and for س as in the instance of جُثْمَانٌ and جُسْمَانٌ; and for other letters. (TA in the latter place.)

A2: [As a numeral, it denotes Five hundred.]

entries.lexicon_en: Habib Anthony Salmone, An Advanced Learner's Arabic-English Dictionary (1889)
entries.lexicon_ar: حبيب سالمون، قاموس عربي انجليزي متقدم للمتعلمين
entries.body_html:
ث
ث
a. Tha The fourth letter of the alphabet. Its numerical value is Five Hundred, (
500 ).
